artifacts.summary: Official University of Exeter central guidance and LibGuides pages were sufficient for policy claims on assessment tiers, recording and referencing, postgraduate research use, and PGR declaration requirements. The linked AI policy PDF was discovered but rejected because it redirected to Microsoft sign-in and could not be fetched without authentication.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Responsible use of AI in research
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Yes, you can use artificial intelligence (AI) as part of your research. It must however be used in line with the University of Exeter Artificial Intelligence policy, the Doctoral College regulations on the use of AI in research assessments, and the University guidance on the use of AI, Enabling AI at Exeter. The key principle is that any use of AI must be accompanied by critical scrutiny and research oversight, because you, as the researcher, remain responsible for any research outputs and assessments.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Should I use AI in my research? / How should I use AI in research?
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Research training and skills | Doctoral College | University of Exeter
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: From 1 August 2024, all Postgraduate Researchers (PGR) must include a statement in their upgrade portfolio and final thesis. This statement must confirm if and how they have used Generative AI (GenAI) in their work. If you do not include this statement, the University will assume you have not used any GenAI tools. The statement must be placed at the end of the upgrade or thesis/dissertation documents, before the reference list.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Enabling AI at Exeter | Strategies | University of Exeter
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The University of Exeter’s Artificial Intelligence policy sets out clear principles for using AI at Exeter. It supports innovation while ensuring fairness, transparency and compliance with data protection and ethical standards. All staff, students and researchers should refer to the policy when using, developing or procuring an AI tool for University purposes. The AI Catalogue is a dynamic list of approved AI tools for staff, students and researchers.

artifacts.title: University of Exeter AI Policy Staged Crawl Summary
artifacts.summary: Staged OpenClaw run for the University of Exeter using official central guidance, library assessment and referencing pages, and Doctoral College postgraduate research guidance. The linked AI policy PDF was discovered but rejected because it redirected to Microsoft sign-in and could not be fetched without authentication.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
